reasoning: Based on the Wikipedia page for 'FIDE rankings' on 2025-03-02, Gukesh D's rating is 2787. A 1% increase would require a mathematical threshold of ~2814.87, meaning he needs a classical FIDE rating of at least 2815. Reaching 2815 is an incredibly rare feat achieved by only about 10 players in history. In the ultra-short term (March 9 and April 1), the probabilities approach zero due to the lack of major classical tournaments allowing a ~28-point gain, coupled with the fact that FIDE only updates rankings monthly. The May 31 date also comes before the finish of his next major confirmed classical event, Norway Chess (ending June 6). Looking further ahead to August 2025 and March 2026, he has more time to improve and accumulate rating points. As a Young World Champion, he has an upside to reach historical heights, but crossing the 2815 barrier still demands a sequence of remarkably dominant tournament performances. Thus I estimate slim odds expanding up to 12% at the 1-year mark.
